title: Ceraia batanensis
taxonomy: Ceraia batanensis belongs to the family of orchids, specifically within the genus Ceraia. It is a species of orchid native to the region of Taiwan. The classification of Ceraia batanensis includes being in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Liliopsida. Further, it is classified as an order Asparagales and a family Orchidaceae. This classification provides a clear understanding of its botanical position.

intro: Ceraia batanensis, also known as Batanes vine or Batanes orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Ceraia batanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
